articles.title: Reimagining Museum Storytelling with Immersive XR
articles.summary: How museums and galleries are using VR and AR to turn static exhibits into interactive, narrative-driven journeys.
articles.content: Museums around the world are embracing XR to extend their narratives beyond the glass case. From room-scale VR recreations of historical events to AR layers that reveal hidden details on artifacts, immersive technologies are transforming passive visits into participatory experiences. In this article we break down successful concepts, hardware considerations, visitor flow design, and accessibility best practices for curators planning their first XR pilot.
articles.author: Dana Morales

articles.title: Designing AR Gallery Tours That Don’t Distract from the Art
articles.summary: A practical guide for galleries using mobile AR and headsets to enhance, not overshadow, exhibitions.
articles.content: When introducing AR into gallery spaces, curators often worry that screens will pull attention away from the art itself. The key is to design a companion experience: light-touch overlays, context-aware prompts, and audio-first storytelling that supports the work on the wall. This guide explores optimal interaction patterns, device policies, and analytics for AR gallery tours, with examples from pilot programs across Europe and North America.
articles.author: Leah Anders

articles.title: From Static Exhibit to Immersive Installation: A 6-Month Roadmap
articles.summary: Timeline, roles, and budget checkpoints for turning a traditional exhibition into an immersive XR installation.
articles.content: Transitioning from traditional vitrines to immersive XR spaces doesn’t have to be overwhelming. In this roadmap we outline a practical six-month plan: discovery and stakeholder alignment, concept prototyping, content production, technical integration, on-site testing, and launch. We highlight decision points unique to museums, like conservation concerns, visitor safety, and staffing models.
articles.author: Dana Morales

articles.title: XR for Open-Air Festivals and Temporary Exhibitions
articles.summary: Tech, logistics, and creative concepts for XR activations at festivals and pop-up exhibitions.
articles.content: Temporary exhibitions and festivals introduce unique XR challenges: weather, connectivity, variable light, and throughput. This article outlines deployment patterns for AR scavenger hunts, projection-mapped stages, and pop-up VR domes, with a focus on quick setup and teardown, rugged hardware, and staff training.
articles.author: Marcus Li

articles.title: VR ‘Try Before You Buy’: What Actually Works in Retail
articles.summary: Lessons from VR try-on and product visualization pilots across fashion, furniture, and automotive retail.
articles.content: Retailers have experimented with VR fitting rooms, virtual showrooms, and 1:1 product visualizations for years. Some pilots quietly disappear; others become core to the customer journey. We analyze where VR adds clear value, how to set realistic KPIs, and what operational changes are required—from staff training to merchandising—to make VR a repeatable part of retail strategy.
articles.author: Nina Patel

articles.title: In-Store AR Navigation: Guiding Shoppers Without Overwhelming Them
articles.summary: How to use AR wayfinding, product discovery, and contextual offers without cluttering the aisle.
articles.content: AR navigation can shorten time-to-product and surface relevant offers at the right moment, but poorly designed flows can frustrate shoppers. We share UX patterns for AR wayfinding, discuss hardware options from smartphones to smart glasses, and explore how retailers are measuring impact on basket size and dwell time.
articles.author: Nina Patel

articles.title: Phygital Pop-ups: Blending XR Installations with Retail Merchandising
articles.summary: How brands are using immersive tunnels, interactive zones, and photo booths to turn pop-up stores into content engines.
articles.content: Pop-up retail has evolved from simple product displays to highly produced, camera-ready experiences. We walk through three recent campaigns that combined interactive XR zones with live merchandising, explaining how they justified budgets, coordinated with landlords, and captured user-generated content at scale.
articles.author: Marcus Li

articles.title: Your First XR Strategy in 60 Days: A Beginner’s Playbook
articles.summary: A step-by-step framework for teams launching their first XR initiative, from discovery to pilot launch.
articles.content: If you are new to XR, it’s tempting to chase flashy demos instead of building a grounded strategy. This playbook walks beginners through a 60-day process: mapping stakeholder goals, prioritizing use cases, choosing between AR, VR, and MR, building a pilot backlog, and setting success metrics. It’s designed to complement our beginner XR strategy workshops with practical templates you can reuse internally.
articles.author: Elena Rossi

articles.title: XR Strategy for Museums: Start Small, Think Long-Term
articles.summary: How museum leaders can align immersive pilots with long-term digital strategy and funding cycles.
articles.content: Museums face unique constraints: grant-based funding, seasonal tourism, and strict conservation policies. We outline how to frame XR initiatives in language boards and funders understand, how to choose the right pilot scale, and how to integrate XR into a broader digital roadmap rather than treating it as a one-off experiment.
articles.author: Leah Anders

articles.title: XR Strategy for Retail Executives: Pilots, Playbooks, and Pitfalls
articles.summary: A concise guide for retail leaders evaluating XR investments across stores, e-commerce, and marketing.
articles.content: Retail executives are under pressure to innovate while protecting margins. We outline a pragmatic approach to XR: how to evaluate store vs. at-home experiences, when to prioritize AR over VR, how to co-fund pilots with brand partners, and which metrics to track beyond footfall and sales uplift.
articles.author: Nina Patel

articles.title: VR Safety Training 101: Building Safer Workplaces with Immersive Practice
articles.summary: An overview of how VR safety training improves retention and reduces incident rates across manufacturing and logistics.
articles.content: VR safety training allows employees to rehearse hazardous scenarios in a controlled environment. We break down core modules—lockout/tagout, working at height, and emergency response—along with benchmarks for evaluating providers, typical pricing models, and tips for rolling out to cohorts of 50–500 learners.
articles.author: Carlos Mendes

articles.title: An AR Playbook for Product Launch Campaigns
articles.summary: How to plan AR-powered product launches that reach over 100K consumers without blowing the budget.
articles.content: From WebAR try-ons to location-based scavenger hunts, AR has become a staple of high-impact product launches. We explain how to choose the right concept, estimate reach vs. budget, and coordinate with media buying so that your AR experience is discoverable and measurable.
articles.author: Marcus Li

articles.title: White-Label XR Production: A Guide for Creative Agencies
articles.summary: How agencies can partner with XR studios to deliver immersive work under their own brand.
articles.content: Many creative agencies are fielding XR briefs without in-house production teams. This guide explains white-label production models, budget thresholds (typically starting around $60,000), IP and credit considerations, and how to structure long-term partnerships that span multiple regions such as the US and EU.
articles.author: Elena Rossi

articles.title: Choosing Your XR Tech Stack in 2025
articles.summary: An overview of current XR hardware and software options for enterprise and cultural organizations.
articles.content: The XR ecosystem is evolving quickly. We compare leading headsets, AR frameworks, and content pipelines, with recommendations based on security requirements, deployment scale, and IT support capacity.
articles.author: Carlos Mendes

articles.title: Designing XR Photo Booths People Actually Want to Share From
articles.summary: Best practices for creating photo booth experiences that drive organic social reach at events and retail pop-ups.
articles.content: Photo booths remain one of the most efficient ways to turn visitors into brand advocates. We explore 360° capture, AR lenses, and green-screen setups, and discuss how to balance queue length, throughput, and content quality.
articles.author: Marcus Li

articles.title: Measuring ROI on Immersive Training Programs
articles.summary: A framework for evaluating the impact of VR and AR training across safety, onboarding, and soft skills.
articles.content: Immersive training is often sold on engagement, but budget holders demand hard numbers. We outline how to measure behavioral change, error reduction, and time-to-competency, with sample dashboards and data collection techniques.
articles.author: Carlos Mendes

jobs.title: UX Designer – XR Products (Mid-Level)
jobs.description: We’re looking for a mid-level UX Designer to shape interfaces and interactions for our VR and AR products across training, retail, and cultural experiences.
jobs.responsibilities: Collaborate with product managers, 3D artists, and engineers to design user journeys for XR applications. Produce user flows, wireframes, and interaction specs for VR and AR. Conduct usability testing with head-mounted displays. Iterate designs based on qualitative and quantitative feedback.
jobs.requirements: 3–5 years of professional UX design experience, including at least 2 years working on XR, gaming, or 3D interfaces. Strong portfolio showcasing interaction design for AR/VR or immersive experiences. Proficiency with Figma or similar tools and familiarity with Unity or Unreal workflows.

jobs.title: Product Designer – XR Platforms (Mid-Level)
jobs.description: Join our design team to define end-to-end product experiences for our XR training and marketing platforms.
jobs.responsibilities: Own design for key product areas, from discovery through detailed interaction design. Collaborate with engineering on implementation for WebXR and native applications. Create prototypes for VR and AR flows and document patterns in our XR design system.
jobs.requirements: 3+ years of product design experience with shipped digital products. Experience designing for 3D or immersive interactions is strongly preferred. Ability to present and explain design decisions to non-design stakeholders.

jobs.title: Senior UX Lead – Immersive Experiences
jobs.description: Lead the UX discipline across complex XR programs spanning training, retail, and cultural installations.
jobs.responsibilities: Define UX vision and standards for XR projects. Mentor a team of UX and interaction designers. Partner with strategy and engineering to scope and deliver multi-track initiatives.
jobs.requirements: 7+ years of UX experience, including 3+ years in XR or 3D interactive environments. Demonstrated leadership on multi-disciplinary teams.

jobs.title: Junior UX Researcher – XR
jobs.description: Support research initiatives across our XR portfolio, from safety training to museum installations.
jobs.responsibilities: Assist with planning and running usability tests in VR and AR. Help synthesize insights into actionable recommendations for design and product teams.
jobs.requirements: 1–2 years of UX research or related experience. Interest in immersive technologies and human–computer interaction.

jobs.title: 3D Technical Artist – VR Environments
jobs.description: Bridge art and engineering to deliver performant VR environments for training and marketing experiences.
jobs.responsibilities: Optimize 3D assets for real-time rendering. Implement shaders and lighting setups in Unity/Unreal. Collaborate with designers to maintain visual fidelity across target platforms.
jobs.requirements: 3–5 years of experience as a technical artist in games or XR. Strong understanding of performance profiling on real-time engines.

jobs.title: Unity Engineer – Training Simulations
jobs.description: Develop VR training simulations for safety, operations, and soft skills programs.
jobs.responsibilities: Implement core gameplay and interaction systems for VR training modules. Integrate analytics and LMS connectors. Collaborate with designers to refine interaction patterns.
jobs.requirements: 3+ years of experience with Unity and C#. Prior experience shipping VR applications is required.

jobs.title: Producer – Immersive Installations
jobs.description: Produce large-scale immersive installations and pop-up experiences in global markets.
jobs.responsibilities: Own project schedules and budgets. Coordinate internal teams and external vendors for on-site builds, logistics, and operations.
jobs.requirements: 4–6 years of production experience in experiential, live events, or XR. Comfortable managing budgets of $250k+.

jobs.title: Operations Manager – XR Events & Logistics
jobs.description: Manage logistics for XR events, workshops, and pop-up installations across multiple regions.
jobs.responsibilities: Plan and coordinate shipping, hardware inventory, and on-site staffing for XR activations. Develop playbooks to streamline repeatable deployments.
jobs.requirements: 3–5 years in operations or event production. Experience with international shipping and customs is a plus.

jobs.title: Marketing Manager – B2B XR Solutions
jobs.description: Drive demand for our XR training, marketing, and installation services with an emphasis on enterprise clients.
jobs.responsibilities: Plan and execute campaigns, manage content calendar, and collaborate with sales on lead generation programs.
jobs.requirements: 4–7 years of B2B marketing experience. Familiarity with XR or enterprise SaaS products is preferred.

jobs.title: Sales Director – Enterprise XR Solutions
jobs.description: Lead enterprise sales for our XR training and immersive marketing solutions in North America and Europe.
jobs.responsibilities: Own new business revenue targets, manage key accounts, and build a small team of account executives.
jobs.requirements: 8+ years in enterprise sales, with at least 3 years selling complex digital or XR solutions.

jobs.title: People Operations Generalist
jobs.description: Support HR operations, recruitment, and culture initiatives across our growing global XR team.
jobs.responsibilities: Manage onboarding, benefits administration, and support recruitment for design, engineering, and production roles.
jobs.requirements: 3–5 years in HR or people operations at a fast-growing organization.

jobs.title: Finance Analyst – Creative Studio
jobs.description: Join our finance team to support project forecasting, pricing models, and reporting.
jobs.responsibilities: Assist with building financial models for XR projects, analyze utilization, and prepare monthly reports.
jobs.requirements: 1–3 years of experience in finance or consulting. Interest in creative and technology industries.

jobs.description: Lead the creative vision for large-scale XR marketing and brand experience programs.
jobs.responsibilities: Set creative direction, pitch concepts to clients, and oversee execution across design and production teams.
jobs.requirements: 10+ years of creative leadership in experiential, advertising, or XR studios. Strong portfolio of flagship work.

service_categories.name: Training & Simulation
service_categories.description: Immersive VR and AR training solutions for safety, operations, onboarding, and soft skills.
service_categories.intro_content: Our Training & Simulation team builds high-impact VR and AR experiences that let people practice real-world scenarios in safe, controlled environments. From OSHA-aligned safety modules to bespoke role-play simulations, we help organizations upskill their workforce at scale.

service_categories.name: Marketing & Brand Experiences
service_categories.description: AR and XR campaigns for product launches, brand activations, and retail experiences.
service_categories.intro_content: We concept and deliver XR-powered brand stories that audiences remember and share. From AR product launch campaigns reaching hundreds of thousands of people to in-store experiential zones, our team blends creativity, technology, and measurable performance.

service_categories.name: Pop-up Installations
service_categories.description: Configurable immersive pop-up installations and touring experiences.
service_categories.intro_content: Use our Pop-up Installations configurator to design immersive experiences for festivals, retail, and one-off events. Combine interactive zones, photo booths, and lounge areas to create a bespoke installation with real-time budget estimates.

case_studies.title: VR Safety Training for Retail Distribution Warehouses
case_studies.summary: A large retail chain reduced incident rates by 32% across three regional distribution centers using VR safety simulations.
case_studies.challenge: Northline operated multiple high-throughput warehouses with rising minor incidents and inconsistent safety training quality across locations.
case_studies.solution: We designed a series of VR safety modules covering pedestrian–forklift interactions, pallet stacking, and emergency evacuations. The content aligned with OSHA standards and ran on standalone headsets to simplify deployment.
case_studies.results: Within six months, incident rates fell significantly and managers reported higher engagement with mandatory safety training.
metrics: 32% reduction in recordable safety incidents in 6 months
94% training completion rate within first rollout wave
87% of staff reported higher confidence in handling emergencies

case_studies.title: VR Flagship Store Preview for a Sportswear Brand
case_studies.summary: An immersive VR walkthrough let VIP customers explore a new flagship store before opening day.
case_studies.challenge: Velocity wanted to build buzz for its new flagship store but the space was still under construction during the global press tour.
case_studies.solution: We created a high-fidelity VR replica of the store, allowing buyers and media to walk through planned merchandising zones, test interactive displays, and preview launch-day activations.
case_studies.results: The VR experience generated strong pre-launch coverage and helped the brand validate store layouts before physical build-out was complete.
metrics: 1,200+ VR walkthroughs completed in 3 weeks
18% increase in pre-orders from key wholesale partners
Coverage secured in 15 industry publications

case_studies.title: VR Emergency Room Simulation for Hospital Staff
case_studies.summary: A series of VR simulations helped ER staff practice high-pressure scenarios without impacting patient flow.
case_studies.challenge: MetroCare needed a way to train multidisciplinary ER teams on rare but critical scenarios without disrupting day-to-day operations.
case_studies.solution: We built branching VR scenarios covering stroke triage, mass-casualty incidents, and cardiac arrest response, with analytics dashboards for educators to review team performance.
case_studies.results: Staff demonstrated faster decision-making in drills and reported feeling better prepared for complex emergencies.
metrics: 21% faster average decision time in simulation assessments
96% of participants rated the VR training as more engaging than traditional drills
Rolled out to 5 hospitals within 4 months

case_studies.title: VR STEM Discovery Lab for Secondary Schools
case_studies.summary: A VR lab program brought physics and biology concepts to life for students across a national school network.
case_studies.challenge: Teachers wanted to make abstract STEM topics more tangible but lacked access to specialized lab equipment.
case_studies.solution: We created a modular VR lab experience covering topics like electromagnetism, cell biology, and planetary motion, mapped to curriculum standards and accessible via portable VR kits.
case_studies.results: Students showed higher test scores in covered units and teachers reported increased classroom participation.
metrics: 14% average improvement in end-of-unit test scores
92% of teachers said VR increased student participation
Deployed to 38 schools in the first academic year

case_studies.title: Time-Travel VR Gallery for a National Museum
case_studies.summary: Visitors stepped into a VR gallery that reconstructed a historic city across three eras.
case_studies.challenge: The museum wanted to attract younger audiences and re-use existing archival material in a more immersive way.
case_studies.solution: We combined photogrammetry, archival photos, and narrative scripting to build a room-scale VR experience where visitors could move between three time periods of the same city square.
case_studies.results: The VR gallery became one of the museum’s most shared experiences on social media and extended average visit time.
metrics: Average visit time to the gallery increased by 27%
Over 50,000 visitors experienced the VR gallery in the first 6 months
Ranked #1 in visitor satisfaction surveys for temporary exhibits

case_studies.title: AR Try-On Campaign for a Global Beauty Product Launch
case_studies.summary: A WebAR try-on experience helped a beauty brand reach over 1.2M consumers during a new product launch.
case_studies.challenge: Lumine needed a way to let consumers try a new cosmetics line without requiring app downloads or in-store testers.
case_studies.solution: We built a WebAR try-on that launched from social ads, QR codes, and the e-commerce site, supporting multiple skin tones and lighting conditions with realistic rendering.
case_studies.results: The campaign significantly lifted engagement and drove measurable add-to-cart increases.
metrics: 1.2M+ unique AR sessions during the 8-week launch window
28% higher add-to-cart rate among AR users vs. non-AR users
Average dwell time of 3.1 minutes in the AR experience

case_studies.title: AR Companion App for a Modern Art Exhibition
case_studies.summary: An AR companion layered artist commentary and hidden animations onto a contemporary art exhibition.
case_studies.challenge: The gallery wanted to add richer context to minimalist works without cluttering walls with text panels.
case_studies.solution: We designed a mobile AR companion that revealed audio commentary, 3D sketches, and process videos when visitors scanned artworks, with accessibility features like transcripts and adjustable text size.
case_studies.results: Visitors spent more time with individual works and the gallery saw higher repeat visitation during the exhibition run.
metrics: 65% of visitors downloaded or accessed the AR companion
Average interaction time per artwork increased by 40%
4.6/5 average rating in post-visit surveys

case_studies.title: Mixed Reality Showroom for Electric Vehicle Launch
case_studies.summary: A mixed reality showroom let customers configure and step around a life-size EV without a physical car present.
case_studies.challenge: Aurora needed a flexible, scalable way to showcase a new electric vehicle in small retail footprints and pop-up spaces.
case_studies.solution: We built a HoloLens-based mixed reality experience that projected a full-scale car into the space, allowing customers to change colors, trims, and view internal components.
case_studies.results: The MR showrooms increased lead capture and helped dealers pre-sell vehicles before inventory arrived.
metrics: 4x increase in qualified leads vs. traditional showroom displays
35% of MR sessions resulted in a test-drive booking
Deployed to 60+ dealer locations globally

case_studies.title: XR Pop-Up Installation at a City Music Festival
case_studies.summary: A multi-zone XR pop-up combined an immersive tunnel, interactive game zone, and photo booth to drive social sharing.
case_studies.challenge: The festival wanted a hero activation that sponsors could brand while offering visitors a memorable, shareable moment.
case_studies.solution: We designed a modular installation with an LED immersive tunnel, a branded interactive game zone, and a 360° photo booth, optimized for high throughput over three festival days.
case_studies.results: The activation became one of the most photographed spots at the event and exceeded sponsor engagement targets.
metrics: 18,000+ visitors through the installation over 3 days
24,000+ pieces of user-generated content shared with festival hashtag
Sponsor satisfaction score of 4.8/5

case_studies.title: VR Lockout/Tagout Training for Manufacturing Plants
case_studies.summary: A series of VR modules standardized lockout/tagout training across multiple manufacturing sites.
case_studies.challenge: PrimeForge needed consistent, auditable safety training across plants in three countries with varying levels of local training quality.
case_studies.solution: We developed VR lockout/tagout simulations for common machines, with multi-language support and LMS integration for tracking completions and assessment scores.
case_studies.results: Audit findings improved, and near-miss reporting indicated safer behavior on the shop floor.
metrics: 98% completion rate across 2,400 employees
40% reduction in lockout/tagout-related near misses
Training time per employee reduced by 25%

case_studies.title: VR Empathy Training for Patient-Centered Care
case_studies.summary: Nurses experienced care journeys from a patient’s perspective through VR scenarios, improving bedside communication.
case_studies.challenge: Evergreen wanted to embed patient-centered care principles into everyday practice, beyond traditional classroom training.
case_studies.solution: We created VR scenarios placing clinicians in the role of patients navigating hospital stays, including communication gaps, noise, and mobility constraints, followed by guided reflection prompts.
case_studies.results: Post-training surveys and mystery patient audits showed improved communication behaviors on wards.
metrics: 19% improvement in patient satisfaction scores on communication items
90% of participating staff recommended the training to peers
Rolled out to 1,100 clinicians within 9 months

service_packages.name: Pop-Up Installation Base Package – Urban Events
service_packages.short_summary: A configurable base structure for immersive pop-up activations in major cities.
service_packages.description: This package includes core infrastructure, staffing, and onsite technical support. It is designed to be combined with interactive zones and photo booths via our configurator.
service_packages.objectives: Provide a predictable starting point for pop-up experiences while allowing flexibility through add-on modules.
service_packages.inclusions: Structural design, basic lighting and sound, event operations playbook, and 3 event days of onsite support.
service_packages.location_constraints: Available in North American and European cities; permits and venue rental not included.

service_packages.short_summary: A turnkey touring installation that travels between 4–6 cities with modular XR zones.
service_packages.description: We design and produce a touring XR installation with reusable scenic elements, interactive zones, and photo booths, optimized for quick load-in and load-out.
service_packages.objectives: Scale a hero experiential concept across multiple markets while controlling production costs.
service_packages.inclusions: Experience design, fabrication, technical production, logistics coordination, and onsite teams for each city.
service_packages.location_constraints: Available in North America, Europe, and select APAC markets.

service_packages.name: Immersive Photo Booth Pop-Up
service_packages.short_summary: A compact pop-up centered around a high-throughput XR photo booth experience.
service_packages.description: Ideal for malls, festivals, and brand roadshows, this package focuses on a single hero photo booth with branded scenes and instant sharing.
service_packages.objectives: Generate user-generated content at scale and capture leads in a compact footprint.
service_packages.inclusions: Photo booth hardware, 3 branded scenes, social sharing setup, and on-site operator for 2–3 days.
service_packages.location_constraints: Available in North America and Europe; ideal as an add-on to larger events.

service_packages.name: Beginner XR Strategy Workshop – Onsite or Remote
service_packages.short_summary: A 1-day beginner-level workshop to help teams shape their first XR strategy and pilot roadmap.
service_packages.description: We guide cross-functional teams through XR fundamentals, use-case prioritization, and pilot planning, using frameworks from our 60-day playbook.
service_packages.objectives: Align stakeholders on XR goals, identify high-value use cases, and define a realistic pilot plan.
service_packages.inclusions: Pre-work survey, 1-day facilitated workshop, template toolkit, and follow-up Q&A session.
service_packages.location_constraints: Remote delivery available globally; onsite available in North America and Europe.

service_packages.name: VR Safety Microlearning Subscription
service_packages.short_summary: Ongoing access to a library of short VR safety modules for continuous learning.
service_packages.description: Organizations can subscribe to our library of 10–20-minute VR safety scenarios that refresh quarterly, supporting just-in-time learning.
service_packages.objectives: Maintain high safety awareness year-round with lightweight, repeatable training moments.
service_packages.inclusions: Access to VR library, deployment toolkit, quarterly content updates, and usage analytics.
service_packages.location_constraints: Available globally for organizations with their own VR hardware.

events.title: Beginner XR Strategy Workshop: From Ideas to 60-Day Roadmap
events.description: A live, beginner-friendly XR strategy workshop for teams taking their first steps into VR and AR. We cover fundamentals, use-case brainstorming, and a practical 60-day pilot planning framework aligned with our "Your First XR Strategy in 60 Days" playbook.

events.title: XR Strategy Lab: Prioritizing Use Cases and Pilots
events.description: An intermediate-level lab for teams that have already run at least one XR pilot and want to prioritize their next wave of use cases across training, marketing, and operations.

events.title: Intro to XR Strategy for Retail & Museums
events.description: A cross-industry beginner session focused on how retail brands and museums can frame their first XR pilots, secure stakeholder buy-in, and choose between AR and VR.
events.location: New York, NY, USA & online streaming

events.title: Advanced XR Roadmapping for Enterprise Leaders
events.description: A two-day, advanced-level program for enterprise leaders building multi-year XR roadmaps across training, marketing, and operations.

events.title: VR Safety Training Instructor Certification
events.description: A hands-on certification course for internal trainers who will facilitate VR safety programs in manufacturing and logistics environments.

events.title: Design Clinic: VR Soft Skills Scenarios for Managers
events.description: A practical clinic for L&D teams to learn how to design VR soft skills scenarios for frontline and mid-level managers using branching conversations.

events.title: AR Product Launch Masterclass: From Concept to 150K+ Reach
events.description: A deep-dive masterclass on planning and executing AR product launches, including concept frameworks, reach modeling, and performance measurement.

events.title: XR for Retail Summit: In-Store, E-Commerce, and Pop-Ups
events.description: A one-day summit for retail leaders exploring XR across physical stores, e-commerce, and pop-up activations, featuring case studies and live demos.

events.title: Pop-Up Installations Configurator Lab: New York Edition
events.description: An in-person lab where brand and agency teams prototype pop-up installations using our configurator, testing different module combinations and budgets.

events.title: Operating Touring XR Pop-Up Installations
events.description: A practical workshop for operations and production teams covering logistics, staffing, and risk management for touring XR pop-ups.

events.title: XR for Museums & Exhibitions Roundtable
events.description: An informal roundtable for museum and gallery leaders sharing lessons from XR pilots, including AR companions, VR galleries, and pop-up installations.

events.title: Executive Briefing: The 2026 XR Landscape
events.description: A fast-paced executive briefing on the XR hardware, software, and market trends that matter for the next 24 months.
